article_title: Scapia Raises $63 Million Series C Led by General Catalyst to Scale India's AI-Powered Travel Fintech
summary: Submission approvable with 2 minor warning(s) — file a corrections record on merge

content_preview.title: Scapia Raises $63 Million Series C Led by General Catalyst to Scale India's AI-Powered Travel Fintech
content_preview.summary: Bengaluru startup Scapia has closed a $63M Series C led by General Catalyst at a post-money valuation exceeding $500 million, more than doubling its $200 million valuation from April 2025.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Bengaluru-based travel fintech Scapia has raised $63 million in a Series C round led by General Catalyst, with existing backers Peak XV Partners and Z47 also participating. The all-equity round values the four-year-old startup at more than $500 million — more than doubling its valuation of approximately $200 million set in April 2025, [according to TechCrunch](https://techcrunch.com/2026/05/20/indian-travel-fintech-scapia-more-than-doubles-valuation-to-over-500m-in-a-year/).

## Wha...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured 786-word News article with clear Overview, What We Know, What We Don't Know, and Analysis sections. Writing is professional and neutral throughout. The article correctly avoids sensationalism about the valuation jump and provides useful market context (Niyo, Ixigo, Revolut competitive landscape).
editor_notes.source_verification: All four sources verified from local snapshots. source-0.html.gz (TechCrunch): confirms $63M round, $500M+ post-money valuation, Peak XV and Z47 participation, ~6x flight booking growth, ~8x hotel growth, 7x customer growth, Goteti quote about lounges. source-1.html.gz (BusinessToday): confirms round details, Goteti CEO quote, Neeraj Arora General Catalyst quote, tier-2/tier-3 focus. source-2.html.gz (TheTechPortal): confirms $63M Series C, prior Series B of $40M, co-branded card details, Scapia Pay. source-3.html.gz (Z47): confirms growth metrics (7x customers, ~6x flights, 8x stays, 17,500+ pincodes, 150+ countries), Goteti full quote, Arora quote. The three domains (businesstoday.in, thetechportal.com, z47.com) were not in the allowlist but are legitimate sources for this story; added to config/source_allowlist.txt as part of this review.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Core facts all verified: $63M round led by General Catalyst, $500M+ valuation, doubling from ~$200M in April 2025, Federal Bank and BOBCARD partnership, dual Visa/RuPay card, growth metrics. One minor quote precision issue: article quotes Goteti via Z47 as 'bring more value to customers through more offerings' but the Z47 source reads 'bring more value to our customers through more offerings' (the word 'our' was dropped). This is a very minor paraphrase within quotation marks, correctable via a clarification note. The 'fourth funding round' claim is an inference: TechCrunch says $126M total raised, implying multiple prior rounds; Z47 confirms backing 'since their very first round'. No source explicitly states 'fourth round', but the inference is reasonable and the claim is in the body, not the headline or summary. The automated 'AI self-reference' warning is a false positive — the pattern 'as an ai' matched the phrase 'as an AI-first' which describes Scapia's product philosophy, not the bot referring to itself.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. The analysis section offers editorial perspective without editorializing. Competitive context is presented factually.
editor_notes.originality: No prior Scapia coverage found in the article archive. This is a new topic.
concerns: The article omits 'our' from one Goteti quote sourced to Z47. Article states 'bring more value to customers through more offerings'; Z47 source reads 'bring more value to our customers through more offerings'. This is a minor paraphrase inside quote marks.
recommendations: File a clarification note documenting the dropped 'our' in the Goteti Z47 quote. The correction is cosmetic and does not affect the substance of the claim.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Solid funding announcement piece. All core claims are well-sourced and verified. The single quote precision issue (dropped 'our') is the only editorial concern, warranting APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS with a clarification record. The automated APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS verdict is upheld, though the 'AI self-reference' finding is a false positive that can be disregarded.
